codeReview.summary: The evidence-only follow-up correctly reattaches the feature to the actual typed DB error classification implementation chain. Revalidation confirms commit 665414a owns the runtime-seam adoption in core/src/error.rs and core/src/lib.rs, while commit 1dc940c owns the decode-seam adoption and focused proof in core/src/error.rs, core/src/repository/mod.rs, and core/tests/integration_db.rs; HEAD still passes the focused classifier, conflict, and decode regressions named in the feature contract.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The rust-library-worker skill still frames work around test-first implementation, but this feature was correctly handled as evidence-only revalidation with no code edits. The skill should expose an explicit evidence-revalidation path so workers do not have to mark a justified procedure deviation for audit-only features.

summary: Pass: the repaired evidence points at the real typed-classification runtime and decode-seam commits (665414a and 1dc940c), and revalidation confirms the focused classifier/conflict/decode proofs still pass from that implementation context rather than unrelated RecordId commits.
